mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening residential demand and construction activity, which reduces aggregate demand and equity earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods when HOUST < 1,400 thousand units, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances despite UNRATE rising > 0.3pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, indicating joint supply-side weakness and labor market softening that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with joint supply and labor weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both INDPRO MoM < 0.5% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months, or Rank IC < 0.045 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 0.3% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 5.0%, indicating joint demand weakness and monetary tightness that constrains consumer spending and equity earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 56% of triggered instances despite RSAFS MoM < 0.3% and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100 thousand persons per month over a 3-month rolling average, signaling labor market softening that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when PAYEMS 3-month growth < 100 thousand/month, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 5.5%, the central bank faces pressure to tighten further, creating uncertainty about future rate paths and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 5.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates to below 2.0% annualized in the most recent quarter while housing starts fall below 1,450 thousand units, indicating joint demand weakness across residential and overall economic activity that reduces equity earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1,450 thousand units
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with broad demand deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized and HOUST < 1,450 thousand units, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
